raw_card_text: Arij Abdul Counselor New York, NY 10018 Arij Abdul holds an MA Degree in Psychology/Mental Health Counseling (2014). She has worked with non-profit/non-sectarian organizations and experience in the Child Welfare System since 2014. She has over 9 years of experience working with, but not limited to, strengthening family dynamics, acculturation, addressing domestic violence, sexual, physical, and emotional abuse, psychological disorders, homelessness, and a vast amount of knowledge relating to child abuse, maltreatment, and neglect. She has worked with non-profit/non-sectarian organizations and experience in the Child Welfare System since 2014. She has over 9 years of experience working with, but not limited to, strengthening family dynamics, acculturation, addressing domestic violence, sexual, physical, and emotional abuse, psychological disorders, homelessness, and a vast amount of knowledge relating to child abuse, maltreatment, and neglect. She has provided clinical casework support, therapeutic, and mental health care for youth, adults, couples, and traumatized families. She has been exposed to multiple populations, cultures, religions, legal/socioeconomic statuses, with a focus on the AMEMSA population (Arab, Middle Eastern, Muslim, and South Asian), immigrants, refugees, and the underprivileged while applying trauma and culturally informed skills. She utilizes the Community Resiliency Model (CRM), which focuses on raising awareness of the biological and neurophysiological aspects of trauma and stress on the body, as well as how to utilize biologically based wellness skills to strengthen one’s resiliency. She is an extremely passionate and compassionate counselor. Take the first step to help. I support anxiety, depression, trauma, and life stress that can feel hard to carry alone. Together, we slow things down and make sense of what you’re going through, noticing patterns that keep showing up. We explore ways to respond that feel more manageable, helping you feel more steady, understood, and able to move forward at your own pace. Reaching out can feel like a big step, especially when you’ve been carrying things on your own. You don’t have to have everything figured out to begin. If it feels easier to express yourself in Bengali or Hindi, that space is here too. This can be a place to feel understood and supported, at your own pace. I don't believe much in theoretical concepts but rather in how the dynamics are presented between the dyad when processing and dealing with sensitive personal issues, which ultimately may help us to develop a stage of psychological balance and emotional fulfillment. Diminishing suffering and self defeating behavior by enabling patient to cope with primary frustration and lack of objectivity are, among others, the factors that elicit change and character modification. Enhancing patient's ability to deal with conflicts, in the here and now, is a must, in the ongoing process of healing. Client centeredness, humanistic position, and empathically understanding are important to uncover underlying manifestations of trauma and irrational thinking. Take the first step to help. I’m a Clinical Mental Health Counselor-I based in New York, and I bring both clinical training and lived experience into the work I do with clients. Reaching out for support can feel overwhelming, especially when emotions feel intense, relationships feel strained, or you’ve been carrying pain on your own for a long time. Whether you’re navigating anxiety, trauma, relationship challenges, or patterns that feel difficult to change, you don’t have to do it alone. If you’re looking for a therapist who is warm, engaged, and committed to walking alongside you in your healing process, I’d be honored to connect. My approach is trauma-informed, relational, and collaborative. I integrate Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) to help clients build practical skills for managing distress and have a particular focus on supporting clients experiencing emotion dysregulation, relationship instability, and traits associated with borderline personality disorder. In our work together, we will focus on what matters most to you, whether that’s building coping skills, improving relationships, processing past experiences, or finding a greater sense of stability and self-understanding. That gap between knowing and changing is exactly where I work. I specialize in three areas: people who are capable and stuck, neurodivergent teens and adults navigating a world that wasn't built for how they think, and couples and families where the dynamic has stopped working and nobody's sure why. We are feeling creatures before thinking creatures — and most people have done plenty of talking about the problem. View 9 Photos My approach is explorative and practical — we're not here to process indefinitely, we're here to figure out what's actually going on and build something that works. I use the Highlands Ability Battery to cut through guesswork, and draw on EMDR and other evidence-based tools when they fit the picture. Therapy here is interactive, not passive. If you're coming in as a couple or family, I work with the dynamic, not just the individuals in it. Humans aren't single-cause problems and I don't treat them that way. If you're looking for someone to nod along and ask how that makes you feel, this probably isn't your fit. I am skilled in psychodynamic, cognitive-behavioral, and dialectical behavior therapies. Take the first step to help. I assist patients move through the emotional, behavioral, and psychological challenges that feel too heavy to carry alone and I do it with a trauma‑informed, compassionate, and results‑driven approach. My clinical background spans from acute psychiatric care, substance rehabilitation, to outpatient psychotherapy, giving me the ability to support patients with deeply personal mental‑health needs. I’ve worked teens, adults, and older adults, and I understand how emotional pain shows up differently in every stage of life View 5 Photos My goal is simple: to help you feel safe, understood, and empowered to rebuild from the inside out. My therapeutic approach is rooted in Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT), Motivational Interviewing (MI), trauma‑focused interventions, and psychodynamic principles. I’m not the kind of therapist who sits back and nods. I’m engaged, present, and intentional. I help you understand the “why” behind your patterns, the “how” behind your healing, and the “what now” that moves you forward. If you’re ready for therapy that is warm, direct, clinically grounded, and transformative, I’m here to help you reclaim stability. Take the first step to help. Email John Saunders about this group or call - (551) 465-5043 DBT -Dialectical Behavior Therapy Hosted by John Saunders Our DBT group teaches individuals how to manage emotions, improve relationships, and cope with distress. We focus on mindfulness, distress tolerance, emotion regulation, and interpersonal effectiveness. Our DBT groups are structured like a class, meeting weekly for two hours. We follow a core curriculum. The curriculum focuses on teaching four key skill sets: Mindfulness: Enhances awareness of the present moment and reduces reactivity to thoughts and feelings. Distress Tolerance: Helps individuals cope with intense emotions and difficult situations without making them worse. Emotion Regulation: Teaches skills to understand, manage, and reduce the intensity of negative emotions. Interpersonal Effectiveness: Improves communication, assertiveness, and relationship skills. You might find yourself overthinking your emotions, struggling to feel regulated, carrying deep-seated trauma or loss, or having a hard time accepting yourself fully. Together, we will make space for what you are feeling, build tools for coping, and help you move toward more self-understanding, steadiness, and joy even when life feels hard. My approach is eclectic and responsive to your needs, drawing from CBT, ACT, IFS, narrative therapy, play therapy, mindfulness, and somatic awareness. As a neurodivergent therapist, I understand the pressure to fit into spaces not built for you, and offer a collaborative, affirming space where curiosity and feedback are welcome. I invite you to take this time for yourself. Therapy can be a space where you set down what you have been carrying and simply be, with no masks to wear and no one else's expectations to meet. Together, we can work to make what you carry feel easier to hold, while recognizing that we are all carrying something. Our life histories, traumas, losses, and disappointments can run so deep that you think the only solution is to give up and stay where you are because change seems frightening. I'm confident I’m able to help you have a more joyful life, better relationships, and to create a more optimistic and exciting vision for your future. You can learn the most effective tools and recognize your strengths focusing on yourself in the now. I can teach you how to treat yourself with patience and compassion, while making a real decision to change. This requires learning how to replace negative patterns with positive ones and knowing that change happens one day at a time with small steps. In my studies I have focused on depression, borderline personality disorder, ADHD, self-harm, attachment disorder, substance abuse, anger management, behavioral issues, and self-esteem. My ideal patient is focused on personal growth and healing. I believe one of the bravest things you can do is begin to understand your own history. Even if we cannot change our environment significantly, we can learn to alter the way in which we perceive it. This can make a profound difference, and will help you to live a more fulfilling life. raw_card_text: Gabrielle Jensen Clinical Social Work/Therapist, LCSW New York, NY 10023 Today, life often presents us with an unsettling tension between overwhelming uncertainty or loss, and a relentless demand to be productive, happy, and to have a strong sense of identity or purpose. Whether one is exploring psychotherapy due to crisis, impasse, troubling symptoms, or relational concerns, all people can benefit from a space where they can make meaning of their experiences and thoughts in a collaborative way. Though psychodynamic treatment requires vulnerability and curiosity, it is an opportunity for transformative self-exploration and healing that will be contained in a space of attentive listening and care. Whether one is exploring psychotherapy due to crisis, impasse, troubling symptoms, or relational concerns, all people can benefit from a space where they can make meaning of their experiences and thoughts in a collaborative way. Though psychodynamic treatment requires vulnerability and curiosity, it is an opportunity for transformative self-exploration and healing that will be contained in a space of attentive listening and care. My approach utilizes a psychodynamic lens, emphasizing how one’s past relates to their present emotions, reactions, relationships, or behavioral patterns. I have experience working with adults, children and families practicing trauma-focused therapy. I also have a background in the arts, and am adept at exploring the creative process with my clients. I am attuned to how a person can be affected by intergenerational trauma, racism, sexual or gender prejudices, class dynamics, and other cultural inheritances. As a therapist, I aim to practice treatment with hope, intimate listening, non-judgement, grace, care, and humor when appropriate. Please reach out when you are ready to begin this work, together. Take the first step to help. Fermín LMSW (she, her) Verified by Psychology Today Anxiety, relational stress, and the weight of societal expectations are often quieter than people expect and hard to name. I work with people navigating the gap between how they function and how they actually feel. That might look like feeling angry, people-pleasing that's become exhausting, relationship stress, or expectations that were never yours. I also work with people in acute stress: a job loss, a breakup, a life transition that's harder to land than expected. As a trauma-informed therapist, my approach is about slowing down enough to understand what's actually happening — not just managing symptoms, but tracing the patterns underneath. People who work well here are often the most put-together on the outside, holding it together for everyone while quietly running on empty. If you know there's more to sort through than you've had space to, that's a good place to start. Therapy here is honest, paced, and built around your actual life. I'd be glad to hear what's bringing you in. Call or email Martha V. Maybe this shows up in your relationships, your reactions, or how you see yourself, even when you don’t fully understand why. I work with teens, young adults, and adults navigating anxiety, attachment wounds, emotion dysregulation, and the impact of early experiences. As someone who is neurodivergent, I also support clients navigating a world that may not always feel aligned with how they think, feel, or process. I help you understand the patterns that once protected you but may now feel limiting. They might shut down, act out, or go very quiet. You've tried everything you know how to try — and you're here because you're ready for something different. I work with children and teens navigating anxiety, autism spectrum experiences, ADHD, behavioral challenges, and emotional overwhelm. My approach uses play, creative tools, and genuine trust-building with kids who've learned that adults don't always get them. View 8 Photos Before clinical training, I earned a Master's in Autism Education and a Master's in Forensic Psychology. I understand IEP meetings, diagnostic processes, and how a child can be completely capable and completely misread by their environment. I also spent seven years in the military in counseling and advocacy roles. Those seven years taught me how to earn trust with people who didn't come in expecting to trust anyone — including kids who've been told something is wrong with them so many times they've started to believe it. Reach out and we'll figure out together if this is the right fit. Email Dr. Christine Romano about this group or call - (914) 339-5716 Death Dialogues: Living with Morality Hosted by Dr. Christine Romano Adults navigating grief, loss, or existential anxiety about death who want a structured, honest space to face it alongside others -- without pressure to move on, stay positive, or pretend things feel okay. Most spaces can't hold honest conversation about death without someone trying to redirect, fix, or comfort. Death Dialogues fills that gap -- a compassionate, structured group where adults of all spiritual backgrounds can face mortality, grief, and existential uncertainty together. Each 60-minute session includes structured reflection, open dialogue, and gentle ritual. Sessions follow a weekly theme and are designed to help members feel grounded and connected. They're managing everything: logistics, emotions, caregiving, often without a co-parent or adequate support network. They're competent on the outside and quietly exhausted on the inside. They want to feel less alone, more understood, and more like themselves again. Solo parenting can be deeply isolating. Even when you're managing well by every external measure, the absence of a co-parent, a consistent support system, or a space where your experience is truly understood can make everything heavier. Do you feel alone, isolated, forgotten, or unwanted? I'm happy you're here because nobody deserves to feel that way, and I commend you for taking the first step to prioritizing your mental health by seeking support. I’m passionate about acknowledging the unique experiences of individuals from all gender identities, sexualities, cultures, disabilities, and backgrounds. After all, everyone deserves to feel heard, validated, and accepted. If you're looking for a therapist who just gets it and welcomes you to be your most authentic self, I just may be your gal! My clientele encompasses individuals of all ages and walks of life! I also have experience in neurodiversity. Techniques used in session will differ depending on the client's individual needs, but could include play therapy, CBT, creative arts, Gestalt, and more. I am always willing to listen to a client's ideas and thoughts. I currently do not offer EMDR. I value building strong therapeutic relationships with clients, gaining trust, and providing guidance to life's many obstacles. I'm a proud multi-ethnic woman, LGBTQ+ ally, & strong advocate for those with chronic illness/disabilities. My role as a healer is something I don't take lightly, so I do my best to ensure everyone is provided a safe space to grow. Liberation Psychotherapy is rooted in the belief that mental health is shaped not only by the inner life of the self, but by the social, cultural, political, and economic realities in which people live. Identity profoundly influences how individuals move through the world, and experiences of othering, marginalization, and oppression can create enduring psychological distress and trauma. Over the past two decades as gay men have experienced integration, there are unintended consequences of the decentralization of gay men: Issues emerging around identity, dating, relationships, intimacy, loss/grief, sex/sexuality, work/life balance, family, masculinity, isolation, depression, anxiety, substance use, trauma. Some modalities that I commonly use include Narrative Therapy, Inner Child Work, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Acceptance-Commitment Therapy, and Dialectical Behavioral Therapy. My mission is to use the therapeutic relationship as a way to build connection, strengthen interpersonal skills, and identify dynamics at play in 1 on 1 settings. I am eager to get to know my clients well and to work towards inner connection and self-compassion, as opposed to correction and "fixing" oneself. What I have found in my personal and professional experience is that the more connected and compassionate we are with ourselves, the more connected and compassionate we are to our surroundings. I am a Brown, queer, gender non-conforming, immigrant, psychotherapist. I practice from an anti-oppressive lens that acknowledges the impact of the many systems we navigate under everyday. Naming the impact along with an exploration of the self, can lead us to a deeper understanding of ourselves and our humanity. I utilize a mixed psychodynamic therapeutic approach that might include CBT, DBT and motivational interviewing. My work with you will be holistic, strengths based and trauma informed. You will be centered in the process. I have experience working with individuals from childhood to elders, as well as families. We will work together to process your experiences, figure out what matters to you so that you can propel yourself forward. It might be interpersonal relationships (platonic, romantic, sexual) you're struggling with or just the relationship to yourself. It might be exhausting, but you've found your way here for support. I'm here to do that with you. When you begin therapy with me, I want you to know that our work will be guided by respect, curiosity, and collaboration. As a bilingual (Mandarin) and bicultural therapist, I recognize the impact of cultural influences on identity and relationships. I believe that with empathy and guidance, challenges can be catalysts for personal growth and healing. You don't have to navigate these difficulties alone—positive change is possible with the right support. raw_card_text: Peter Vietze Psychologist, PhD New York, NY 10002 I am in-network for Blue Cross, Cigna, Horizon, Aetna, Medicare, and Medicaid (but not Fidelis, Carelon HealthFirst or MetroPlus). I began practicing in Maryland in 1978. I resumed practicing in New York in 2004 and added an office in New Jersey in 2013. My practice covers a broad age range from 2-90. I am most comfortable treating adults with a variety of mental health issues. I also see couples and do family therapy. In addition, I have done play therapy with children. I am also comfortable conducting psychological evaluations of children ranging in age from a year to 21 years. I am an expert in developmental disabilities including autism spectrum disorders. You are not alone in this journey; I am here to help you every step of the way. I work with teens and adults to create a space where you feel safe, supported, and truly seen. Together, we’ll deepen your self-understanding, break unhelpful patterns, and build healthier ways of coping with life’s challenges. I believe in a collaborative, personalized approach to therapy because everyone’s path to healing looks different. I welcome clients of all identities and backgrounds and strive to lead with warmth, curiosity, and empathy in every session. I specialize in working with eating disorders, anxiety, and life transitions. I also work with those facing challenges like depression, low self-esteem, relationship stress, and school or job pressures. I use a person-centered approach and draw from Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT), and mindfulness-based practices. I know reaching out for help can be one of the hardest, most vulnerable steps you’ll take. I’m so glad you’re here, and I’d be honored to walk alongside you on this path. raw_card_text: Clint C Stankiewicz Verified Verified New York, NY 10007 Despite having reached the pinnacle of my profession after nearly two decades of practice, it was my own struggle with learning disabilities, dual diagnosis and other medical conditions and issues, that changed my perspective and my life. I came to appreciate an approach which focuses on the here and now; solution focused and goal oriented. Reaching out for support takes courage, especially when you're unsure of what to expect. You're here, and that’s a powerful first step! As human beings, we are wired for connection, and healing often begins in the presence of a safe, supportive relationship. In therapy, you can gain a deeper understanding of your experiences, recognize and express your needs, and cultivate more fulfilling relationships with others and with yourself. I offer a safe and supportive space grounded in relationship and trust. My approach is primarily informed by Somatic Experiencing, a model that helps us access the body’s natural ability to heal. Together, we’ll use curiosity and compassion to explore your experiences and support meaningful, lasting change. I know firsthand that the most meaningful parts of our lives can also be the most challenging. I’ve experienced loss, self-doubt, and the complexities of navigating connection and conflict. These experiences deepen my compassion. I believe in your innate resilience and capacity for healing, and I’m here to help you access and trust that inner strength. Take the first step to help. I specialize in providing cognitive behavioral therapy and schema-focused cognitive therapy. I treat a wide variety of problems, such as anxiety disorders (panic disorder, social anxiety, phobias, OCD), depression, low self-esteem, relationship problems, compulsive overeating, weight loss/management, addictions and more. I work with adults, in individual and couples therapy. My approach is solution-oriented, and through the process of our collaborative effort, our overarching goal is to help you to achieve positive change that will help you to feel better and to improve your life. Cognitive behavioral therapy is a scientifically proven method that helps people to correct errors or distortions in their thinking. These negative thoughts/beliefs contribute to negative emotions and poor coping strategies. By correcting the negative thoughts and behaviors, we can help to alleviate negative emotions, and make better life choices. Schema-focused cognitive therapy is a specialized form of cognitive therapy that is used to help people with longstanding maladaptive (negative, problematic) patterns in thinking, feeling and behavior. These patterns typically begin early on, and get perpetuated and reinforced in one's life. I have expertise in this specialized area. I’m a Mental Health Counselor-LP providing therapy for teens, young adults, adults, and mothers at every stage—whether you’re navigating postpartum changes, parenting stress, identity shifts, or work-life balance. I help clients work through anxiety, depression, life transitions, stress management, and relationship issues. Healing is not linear, but every step forward matters—and I’m here to walk that path with you. If you too are now aware of the need to tap into the power of self-knowledge so that you can live a life that helps you find purpose, fulfillment, confidence and competence, your decision to seek therapy is a natural step in the right direction. My role as a therapist is to assist in the process of helping you heal from the emotional “dis-ease” that can be part of the human experience. I am here to help you discover the solutions to the problems you face. Together we can explore issues related to trauma, substance use, processing grief, learning healthy coping skills, improving communication, resolving conflict, managing anxiety, addressing cultural identity concerns and reducing race-based stress. Your healing is fostered in a nurturing and supportive environment. My therapeutic philosophy is that the client is the expert on their life experiences, while I serve as a guide. With my specific knowledge, skills and abilities, I am able to assist with identifying helpful tools that can be used along life’s journey, so that you can better navigate it. Take the first step to help. Let’s Talk Wellness and Not Illness! The goal of my team of licensed mental health clinicians is to provide person-centered care, that focuses on the many strengths of the children, adolescents, young adults, and the families that we treat. We use evidenced-based approaches to diagnose and provide individual, family and group therapy, as well as psychiatry. We offer our clients the option to have their sessions at our clinic office, at safe spaces in their communities, at their homes and on Zoom. The goal isn’t just to get through—it’s to feel more grounded, more in control, and more like yourself again. You don’t have to keep carrying this on your own. If this resonates with you, we can take the next step together. I help kids identify and utilize coping and social skills so they can better regulate their emotions and positively interact with others. I teach kids how to reframe their negative thoughts with positive ones so they can increase their self-esteem, experience less anxiety, build confidence and form more authentic relationships with peers and adults. I teach parents how to effectively implement behavior strategies, to prevent escalations and to de-escalate their child without becoming reactive, to speak in a way that the child feels heard, to practice more empathetic parenting and re-establish a secure attachment to their child. I also teach parents how to effectively co-parent after divorce. I find great value in helping people, especially older teens & early adults, to understand their identities. I love partnering with my clients to define & develop life goals, find interests, and understand how past experiences may or may not be affecting their daily lives, enabling them to make positive changes that serve them better. I'm looking forward to working with you as you start your therapy journey. Seeking out therapy is a step in moving towards improved emotional and psychological well-being. Therapy can seem scary at first, but it gives you an opportunity to learn more about yourself. The weight of these experiences, often made more painful by external pressures, can be isolating and may reactivate old wounds that never seem to fully heal. I understand what it means to feel out of control and to begin the daunting process of interrupting painful patterns. I work with adolescents and adults, focusing on male-identified persons and those navigating addiction or compulsive patterns. Together we’ll disrupt what no longer serves you and tend to the tender parts within. Using a trauma lens, I help heal old wounds with softness, creating space for growth, connection, and self-nurturing. Many of the feelings and patterns you carry once helped you survive; together, we can find ways to honor the resilience within. Take the first step to help. raw_card_text: Adrianne Alfonso Pre-Licensed Professional, MS, MHC-LP 9 Endorsed New York, NY 10017 Reaching out for mental health support can feel challenging, and finding the right therapist isn’t always easy. I strive to create a supportive, affirming space where you feel seen and understood. If you’re trying to love yourself but feel guilty or unsure where to begin, you’re not alone. I support clients navigating anxiety, relationship dynamics, life transitions, and ways to improve quality of life while honoring cultural and/or spiritual values. Together, we can gently explore grief and loss, including non-death losses that impact identity and connection. raw_profile_text: Adrianne Alfonso, Pre-Licensed Professional, New York, NY, 10017 | Psychology Today Psychology Today Therapists US Log In Sign Up and Get Listed Home New York New York Adrianne Alfonso Pre-Licensed Professional, MS, MHC-LP (914) 685-8722 or Email Me Adrianne Alfonso Pre-Licensed Professional, MS, MHC-LP (she, her) Verified by Psychology Today 9 Endorsed Reaching out for mental health support can feel challenging, and finding the right therapist isn’t always easy. I strive to create a supportive, affirming space where you feel seen and understood. If you’re trying to love yourself but feel guilty or unsure where to begin, you’re not alone. I support clients navigating anxiety, relationship dynamics, life transitions, and ways to improve quality of life while honoring cultural and/or spiritual values. Together, we can gently explore grief and loss, including non-death losses that impact identity and connection. My work also involves individuals who are experiencing acculturation issues, loss of family connection, relationship issues, or identity issues. Additionally, if you are a current/former athlete, I am here for you, too! Treating situations such as your journey/struggles as an athlete, whether it is an injury or loss of identity during or post-competing.
